patterns.name: Binary Search
patterns.trigger: Sorted array, find target or boundary. Halve the search space each step.

patterns.name: DFS / Flood Fill
patterns.trigger: Grid or tree traversal, visit connected components, modify in-place.
patterns.skeleton:
def dfs(grid, r, c):
    if out_of_bounds or visited: return
    mark visited
    dfs(grid, r+1, c)
    dfs(grid, r-1, c)
    dfs(grid, r, c+1)
    dfs(grid, r, c-1)

patterns.name: Linked List Reversal
patterns.trigger: Reverse a linked list or portion of it. Three-pointer dance.
patterns.skeleton:
prev = None
while head:
    tmp = head.next
    head.next = prev
    prev = head
    head = tmp
return prev

patterns.name: Two Pointers
patterns.trigger: Sorted array/string, find pair or compare from both ends.
patterns.skeleton:
left, right = 0, len(arr) - 1
while left < right:
    if condition: return
    elif too_small: left += 1
    else: right -= 1

patterns.name: Dummy Node
patterns.trigger: Build a new linked list, or when head might change. Avoids edge cases.
patterns.skeleton:
dummy = ListNode(0)
curr = dummy
# ... build list by setting curr.next
return dummy.next

patterns.name: HashMap + Doubly Linked List
patterns.trigger: O(1) get + put with eviction order (LRU, LFU). Map for lookup, DLL for ordering.
patterns.skeleton:
map: key -> node
dll: head <-> node <-> ... <-> tail
get: lookup map, move node to front
put: add to map + front, if full evict tail

patterns.name: Bottom-Up DP
patterns.trigger: Count ways / min cost to reach end. Overlapping subproblems, optimal substructure.
patterns.skeleton:
dp = [0] * (n + 1)
dp[0], dp[1] = base_cases
for i in range(2, n + 1):
    dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]
return dp[n]

patterns.name: Counting / Frequency Map
patterns.trigger: Compare character/element frequencies. One collection must be subset of another.
patterns.skeleton:
from collections import defaultdict
count = defaultdict(int)
for ch in source: count[ch] += 1
for ch in target:
    count[ch] -= 1
    if count[ch] < 0: return False
return True

patterns.name: Boyer-Moore Voting
patterns.trigger: Find element appearing more than n/2 times. O(n) time, O(1) space.
patterns.skeleton:
candidate, count = None, 0
for num in nums:
    if count == 0: candidate = num
    count += 1 if num == candidate else -1
return candidate

Both iterative and recursive. Tougher than expected.
Tricky — must include mid when recursing left if mid could be the answer. Base case: start == end.
prev starts as None not a dummy node. while head not while head.next. Return prev.
defaultdict(int), decrement for each ransomNote char, return False if count < 0
Two approaches: boolean array 10^6+1 (brute) vs smaller array + chaining (real hash)
Take even portion of all chars (val//2)*2. Add 1 if any odd-frequency char exists (center).
Boyer-Moore voting: candidate + count. Decrement on mismatch, reassign at 0. Survives because majority > n/2.
Tree recursion, track max diameter via DFS height
